title: UPSERT (ON CONFLICT)

overview.what: UPSERT is a combination of 'update' and 'insert'. In Postgres, it's written as INSERT ... ON CONFLICT. If a row with the same unique key already exists, instead of throwing an error, the database can either DO NOTHING or DO UPDATE to modify the existing row.
overview.why: It safely handles race conditions where multiple requests might try to create the same record simultaneously, and it simplifies application logic by merging check-and-create into a single database operation.

why_learning_this: You can write idempotent inserts that never fail on duplicate key errors.
You avoid the inefficient 'SELECT to check, then INSERT or UPDATE' pattern which is vulnerable to race conditions.

common_mistakes.title: Missing unique constraint
common_mistakes.explanation: ON CONFLICT requires a unique constraint or index on the column(s) specified. Without it, the database doesn't know what constitutes a 'conflict'.

common_mistakes.title: Referencing the old row instead of EXCLUDED
common_mistakes.explanation: When doing an ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE, you must use the special EXCLUDED table to refer to the values that were proposed for insertion.

recall_questions.q: What keyword is used in the DO UPDATE clause to refer to the new values that failed to insert?

understanding_checks.query: INSERT INTO customers (id, name, country) VALUES (1, 'Alice V2', 'US') ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET name = EXCLUDED.name RETURNING id, name;
understanding_checks.question: What rows does this query return (assuming 'id' is a primary key)?
understanding_checks.why: Customer id 1 already exists, causing a conflict. The DO UPDATE clause triggers, updating the name to 'Alice V2'.

understanding_checks.query: INSERT INTO products (id, name, category, price) VALUES (1, 'Keyboard', 'Electronics', 49.99) ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING RETURNING id;
understanding_checks.question: What rows does this query return?
understanding_checks.why: Product id 1 exists. DO NOTHING silently ignores the insert, and since no row was inserted or updated, RETURNING yields nothing.

query_walkthrough.query: INSERT INTO products (id, name, category, price) VALUES (2, 'Mouse V2', 'Electronics', 24.99) ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET name = EXCLUDED.name, price = EXCLUDED.price RETURNING id, name, price;
query_walkthrough.focus: The conflict on id=2 triggers an update, merging the EXCLUDED.name and EXCLUDED.price into the existing row.

practice_tasks.title: Upsert an employee salary
practice_tasks.prompt: Given this starter code, modify it to update the employee's salary to the new value if the employee id already exists.
practice_tasks.starter_code:
INSERT INTO employees (id, name, department, salary)
VALUES (6, 'Xavier', 'Sales', 100000)
ON CONFLICT (id) DO ...

challenge.title: Upsert an order
challenge.prompt: Write an upsert query that tries to insert order id 8 for customer 2 on '2023-08-01' with status 'shipped'. If order id 8 already exists, update its status to 'shipped'. Return the id and status.
challenge.solution: INSERT INTO orders (id, customer_id, order_date, status) VALUES (8, 2, '2023-08-01', 'shipped') ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET status = EXCLUDED.status RETURNING id, status;
